title: PTD Today — Media
disclaimer: Informational only — AI-generated summaries; may contain errors. Verify with the original video.

items.title: How I turned $27K of land into $700/night luxury cabin rentals

ai.summary: Based on the available description, in 2015 contractor Chris Broomfield bought a five-acre property in upstate New York intending to build Airbnb rentals. Over three years he commuted from Connecticut to build an A-frame cabin himself, added more cabins, and by September 2025 the property had generated over $2.1 million.
bullets: 2015 purchase of a five-acre property in upstate New York.
Buyer: full-time contractor Chris Broomfield planning Airbnb properties.
Built an A-frame cabin solo over three years with weekend commutes from Connecticut.
Expanded the site with additional cabins over time.
As of September 2025 the property had brought in over $2.1 million.
takeaways: High revenue from a rural short-term rental cluster suggests potential increases in local demand for reliable power and connectivity.
Owner-driven, phased development (multi-year, incremental builds) implies infrastructure needs may grow gradually rather than as a single large spike.
Monitoring concentrated short-term rental growth can help prioritize capacity and resilience planning for rural grid and connectivity providers.

items.title: Restrukturyzacja a podatki. Rozpoznanie, obowiązki, konsekwencje | Monika Palmowska, Tomasz Regulski

ai.summary: Based on the available description… This KPMG ON AIR episode explains how to assess operational changes (e.g., moving production, distribution or teams) from the perspective of transfer pricing and valuations, and how to spot when such changes amount to a restructuring. It also outlines reporting obligations (TPR, MDR), the importance of timely documentation, common valuation errors, and provides practical examples plus a checklist for firms.
bullets: Defines restructuring in transfer pricing and how to recognize material changes, including EBIT analysis.
Explains the role and importance of restructuring documentation and preparing it on time.
Details reporting obligations such as TPR and MDR.
Covers the role of valuations, frequent valuation mistakes, and illustrative examples (production/distribution moves, employee transfers, contract transfers).
Ends with a practical checklist of actions firms should consider.
takeaways: Treat operational moves (e.g., relocating services or assets) as potential TP restructurings and assess financial impact (EBIT) early.
Prepare and retain restructuring documentation promptly to reduce future compliance risk and stress.
Anticipate reporting needs (TPR, MDR) when planning cross-border or structural changes.
Commission robust valuations and avoid common valuation errors to support transfer pricing positions.
Review employee and contract transfers carefully, as they can change business profiles and trigger additional obligations.

items.title: When your goals need backup, Copilot shows up.

ai.summary: Based on the available description, the post is a brief call-to-action telling viewers to download and try Copilot via the link in the account bio. It invites a hands-on trial of the product.
bullets: Single-line call-to-action to download and try Copilot.
Access point specified as the 'link in bio'.
Encourages direct, hands-on testing.
takeaways: If you want to evaluate Copilot for grid or data-center needs, use the link in bio to download and run a trial.
A hands-on trial is the available path here to judge whether Copilot aligns with your operational requirements.
Coordinate any testing with internal procurement, security, and compliance processes before broader deployment.

items.title: The Most Expensive Mistake in AI (And How to Avoid It)

ai.summary: Based on the available description… Marcus Oliver, lead of Google delta (Google’s professional services team), says the most expensive mistake in AI is rushing to code without a strategy. He advocates escaping the “coding trap” by reimagining value chains and deploying multi-agent systems to drive real business outcomes.
bullets: Most expensive AI mistake: rushing to code without a strategy.
Focus should be on reimagining value chains, not just building models.
Deploy multi-agent systems as a means to achieve business outcomes.
Guidance presented by Marcus Oliver, lead of Google delta (professional services).
takeaways: Require clear business value and a strategic plan before provisioning infrastructure for AI projects.
Assess how proposed reimagined value chains will map to data center and operational requirements.
Engage professional services early (e.g., Google delta) to align AI design with infrastructure capabilities.
Evaluate multi-agent system designs for their implications on compute, networking, and management ahead of deployment.

ai.summary: Based on the available description, this replay episode reintroduces the kickoff of PwC’s Reporting reset series and reviews foundational financial statement presentation topics. It covers GAAP and SEC requirements, reporting period selection, balance sheet and income statement presentation, accounting changes and error corrections, and subsequent-event recognition and disclosure, with timestamps and links to PwC guidance.
bullets: Replay of the Reporting reset series kickoff focused on presentation fundamentals.
Segments cover GAAP/SEC requirements, reporting periods, balance sheet, income statement, accounting changes, and subsequent events (with timestamps).
Notable discussion on differences between public and private company financial statements.
Provides links to PwC Financial statement presentation guide chapters for deeper guidance.
Guests: Pat Durbin (PwC Deputy Chief Accountant) and host Diana Stoltzfus; transcripts available on request.
takeaways: Ensure asset and liability classification and disclosures align with GAAP/SEC rules to support accurate capital and performance reporting for grid/data center investments.
Align reporting periods with operational and budgeting cycles so financial results and capacity investments are comparable and useful for planning.
Track accounting changes, estimates, and error-correction processes proactively—these can materially affect reported results and benchmarking for facility performance.
Maintain a process to identify and disclose subsequent events that could affect stakeholders’ view of asset viability, outages, or post-period obligations.
Coordinate finance and operations early to ensure disclosures and presentations reflect technical realities (e.g., capital projects, service interruptions) that matter to investors and regulators.

items.title: DeepKeep: Secure your AI agents and applications end-to-end

ai.summary: Based on the available description… DeepKeep on Google Cloud is presented as a unified platform to secure AI agents, applications, and user interactions by providing visibility to eliminate shadow AI and protect infrastructure from evolving threats. The video highlights three core capabilities—AI Lens for visibility and policy enforcement, AI Red Teaming for stress-testing, and an AI Firewall for real-time prompt/response guardrails.
bullets: AI Lens: visibility into user interactions and policy application
AI Red Teaming: stress-test apps and models against real-world attacks
AI Firewall: real-time prompt and response protection with guardrails
Aims to eliminate shadow AI and protect infrastructure
Available on Google Cloud; visit deepkeep.ai for details
takeaways: Centralized visibility and policy controls help reduce operational risk from unmanaged AI use
Proactive red teaming can uncover vulnerabilities before they impact production systems
Real-time filtering of prompts/responses can limit exposure to data leakage or malicious inputs
Deploying a dedicated AI security layer on Google Cloud supports consistent protection for AI workloads

items.title: Copilot helps Christa find her inner star 🌟

ai.summary: Based on the available description, the video uses a star-filled-sky metaphor to show Copilot helping Christa "find the star within," framing the product as a personal empowerment tool. It ends with a call to action inviting viewers to download and try Copilot via the link in the bio.
bullets: No transcript available; summary is drawn from the description.
Theme: sky full of stars and finding an inner star — Copilot as an empowerment metaphor.
Promotional call-to-action to download and try Copilot (link in bio).
Short, uplifting promotional tone focused on personal uplift.
takeaways: Marketing note: Copilot is positioned as a user-focused, empowerment tool—expect user interest in accessible AI experiences.
Call-to-action present: Copilot is promoted as readily downloadable, so consider evaluating onboarding and trial options for staff.
Messaging emphasizes simplicity and personal impact—prioritize usability and user experience when assessing AI tools for operations.
If exploring AI tools, factor in how consumer-facing expectations could influence internal adoption and support requirements.

items.title: GeForce Garage - Summer of RTX Community Build

ai.summary: Based on the available description… Congrats to r/buildapc contest winner u/JeffersonPutnam — NVIDIA GeForce showcased their Summer of RTX community build featuring a small-form-factor GeForce RTX 5090 system. The video lists the full winning system parts (Fractal Design Era 2 chassis, RTX 5090 Founders Edition, MSI B850I motherboard, Ryzen 7 9800X3D, 32GB DDR5-6000, Corsair SF1000, Corsair Nautilus 240 RS) and links to the PCPartPicker build and GeForce social channels.
bullets: Winner: u/JeffersonPutnam from the Summer of RTX r/buildapc contest
Build type: small-form-factor (Fractal Design Era 2) GeForce RTX 5090 rig
Key components: GeForce RTX 5090 FE, Ryzen 7 9800X3D, MSI MPG B850I Edge, 32GB DDR5-6000
Power and cooling: Corsair SF1000 PSU and Corsair Nautilus 240 RS AIO
Video host: GeForce host Dwight (Twitter: @_MightyPotato); PCPartPicker parts list provided
takeaways: The community build highlights interest in integrating GeForce RTX 5090 into compact, small-form-factor systems.
Combination of high-end GPU with a high-performance CPU (Ryzen 7 9800X3D) and DDR5 memory indicates consumer demand for powerful desktop configurations.
Power and cooling choices (SF1000 PSU, Nautilus 240 RS AIO) demonstrate considerations for delivering high performance in space-constrained builds.
